heading: Legacy Connection Strings
body:
Before 31 March 2020, you wrote Atlas connection strings as
follows:

| AWS (Amazon Web Services) | foo-shard-00-00-ab123.mongodb.net foo-ab123.mongodb.net             |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Azure (Microsoft Azure)   | foo-shard-00-00-ab123.azure.mongodb.net foo-ab123.azure.mongodb.net |
| Google Cloud              | foo-shard-00-00-ab123.gcp.mongodb.net foo-ab123.gcp.mongodb.net     |

If you enabled **Private Only** mode, these hostnames resolve to peered
network IP addresses. If you disabled that mode, hostnames resolve to
public IP addresses.

If your application uses a legacy connection string in **Peering Only**
mode, switch to
[Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).
